full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Finding available papers not already selected by other students
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: I cant select from the selected ones. suggest 5 research papers i can work as per provided pdf and rank them by difficulty and mention method and specifics for the papers
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Claude suggested unselected papers ranked by difficulty, which helped me identify suitable candidates that were not already taken by other students.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I cross-checked the live response sheet and confirmed the paper was not selected by any other student.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Verify whether One-Sided SVR paper satisfies all Part A guidelines
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: is one side vector valid check for every thing
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Claude performed a complete 13-point eligibility check confirming ICML 2010 is CORE A*, method is 100% SVM variant, paper is not in the taken list, and experiments are CPU reproducible on UCI datasets.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I manually verified the ICML 2010 proceedings page and confirmed the paper title, authors, and venue before selecting.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding what the paper does and how it extends standard SVM
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What does One-Sided Support Vector Regression for Multiclass Cost-Sensitive Classification do and how does it extend standard SVM?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Clarified that the paper modifies the standard SVM hinge loss to incorporate asymmetric misclassification costs, making predictions cost-aware rather than accuracy-only. This confirmed the paper is a direct SVM variant.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified by reading the abstract and Section 2 of the paper where the authors explicitly derive the modified loss function from standard one-versus-all SVM.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding failure cases and limitations for Part C preparation
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What are the failure cases and limitations of One-Sided SVR cost-sensitive SVM I should know for the exam?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Identified key failure cases: (1) wrong cost matrix leads to biased predictions, (2) highly imbalanced costs can cause model to always predict the cheapest class, (3) cost matrix must be defined manually and is domain-dependent.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified against the experimental section of the paper where performance degrades with extreme cost ratios.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding the prediction/inference procedure for Part B and C
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: How does inference work in One-Sided SVR cost-sensitive classification step by step?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Explained that inference works by: training one SVM per class with asymmetric cost weights, then predicting the class that minimizes expected cost rather than maximizes margin. Same computational cost as standard SVM at test time.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified against Section 3 of the paper where the prediction rule is explicitly stated.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Checking reproducibility feasibility on a standard laptop
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Can One-Sided SVR cost-sensitive SVM be reproduced on a CPU-only laptop using scikit-learn for the exam?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed that the method is implementable using scikit-learn SVC with class_weight parameter on UCI datasets like Iris, Wine, or Glass — all used in the original paper — with no GPU required.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Checked that UCI Iris and Wine datasets are publicly available and confirmed scikit-learn supports cost-sensitive SVM via class_weight.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Getting the official publisher link for the paper
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: official publisher link for One-Sided Support Vector Regression for Multiclass Cost-Sensitive Classification ICML 2010
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided the official ICML proceedings PDF link https://icml.cc/Conferences/2010/papers/207.pdf confirming it is a formally published ICML paper and not arXiv-only.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Visited the ICML link and confirmed it matches the correct paper title, authors, year, and conference.

top_5_prompts.prompt: How does inference work in One-Sided SVR cost-sensitive classification step by step?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Understanding the inference procedure is critical for Part B and C. This prompt clarified exactly how predictions are made at test time — minimizing expected cost rather than maximizing margin — which I will need to explain and implement without any tools.

top_5_prompts.prompt: What are the failure cases and limitations of One-Sided SVR cost-sensitive SVM I should know for the exam?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Part C will test failure case reasoning without internet access. This prompt helped me identify the three most important failure modes: wrong cost matrix, extreme cost imbalance, and domain-dependence of cost specification. I verified all three against the paper's experimental section.

top_5_prompts.prompt: What does One-Sided Support Vector Regression for Multiclass Cost-Sensitive Classification do and how does it extend standard SVM?
top_5_prompts.why_important: This was essential for confirming the paper qualifies as an SVM variant under the exam rules and for understanding the core contribution deeply enough to explain it in Part C without tools.

top_5_prompts.prompt: is one side vector valid check for every thing
top_5_prompts.why_important: Before committing to this paper I needed a complete eligibility check across all 13 exam rules. This prompt confirmed zero risk on every rule — venue, year, method, reproducibility, and taken list — removing all uncertainty before resubmission.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Can One-Sided SVR cost-sensitive SVM be reproduced on a CPU-only laptop using scikit-learn for the exam?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Reproducibility is my direct responsibility per the exam rules. This prompt confirmed I can use scikit-learn on UCI datasets already used in the paper, making Part B implementation straightforward and low-risk.

student declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Part A.
